channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Roadmap for East Africa to host a successful AFCON 2027
item.description: East Africa, consisting of Kenya, Uganda, and Tanzania, has been officially confirmed by CAF as the host region for AFCON 2027, following Morocco’s highly praised 2025-2026 tournament. Morocco set a h...

item.title: Government begins 5G spectrum auction with telecom stakeholders in Islamabad
item.description: The government of Pakistan has officially kicked off the 5G spectrum auction in Islamabad, with Finance Minister Muhammad Aurangzeb and IT Minister Shaza Fatima Khawaja opening the proceedings. The IT...

item.title: Ethan Sanders shines as Raiders prepare for Warriors without Papali'i
item.description: The Canberra Raiders will head into round two of the NRL season in Auckland without their experienced forward Josh Papali'i due to a concussion. Despite this setback, the squad is upbeat thanks to you...

item.title: Russian and Chinese Hackers Exploited iPhone Toolkit Originally Developed for U.S. Government
item.description: A sophisticated iPhone-hacking toolkit, known as 'Coruna,' was used in widespread attacks targeting users in Ukraine and China. Originally developed for U.S. government intelligence purposes by the mi...

item.title: Cast of 'Heated Rivalry' Addresses Toxic Fan Behavior and Online Harassment
item.description: The cast of the popular queer hockey series 'Heated Rivalry' is confronting negative behavior from parts of its growing fanbase. Hudson Williams and François Arnaud, joined later by Robbie Graham-Kunt...

item.title: Climate change is reshaping skiing accessibility in the Alps
item.description: While skiing continues to attract global attention, climate change is altering the economic and social landscape of winter sports in Alpine regions. Rising temperatures and shorter snow seasons have f...

item.title: Suite Tenant Raises Concerns Over Treatment and Operations at FNB Stadium
item.description: A suite leaseholder at FNB Stadium has voiced strong frustrations about the treatment of hospitality tenants by Stadium Management SA (SMSA). Paying over R600,000 annually in rent, the leaseholder cla...

item.title: Experts and Community Leaders Call for Stronger Environmental Protection in Niger Delta
item.description: Prof. Ibibia Worika, wey be lecturer for University of Port Harcourt and Director of Centre for Advanced Law Research, don call make dem implement environmental laws well-well for Niger Delta. During ...

item.title: Raghav Chadha Calls for More Udaan Yatri Cafes at Indian Airports
item.description: Raghav Chadha, a prominent political figure, has suggested the expansion of 'Udaan Yatri Cafes' across all airports in India. These cafes, aimed at enhancing passenger experience, provide a comfortabl...

item.title: KSE-100 rebounds sharply after Monday’s record drop, trading briefly suspended
item.description: Pakistan’s stock market witnessed an extraordinary early surge on Tuesday as the KSE-100 index jumped by 9,303.75 points, marking a 6.35 per cent increase. This sharp rally triggered an automatic temp...

item.title: Kelowna charity temporarily closes after theft, seeks community support to recover
item.description: A Kelowna-based charity, Mamas for Mamas, has temporarily closed its Karma Market after a break-in and theft occurred early on Friday morning. The Karma Market, a free store offering clothing and esse...

item.title: Urgent Dialysis Services Needed for Mothers with Postpartum Kidney Issues at Muhimbili Hospital
item.description: During the International Women’s Day celebrations, Dr Delilah Kimambo, Executive Director of Muhimbili National Hospital, appealed for urgent measures to support mothers suffering from postpartum acut...

item.title: Senate Passes Bill on Third Reading to Abolish Optical Media Board and Transfer Functions to IPOPHL
item.description: The Senate of the Philippines has approved on third and final reading Senate Bill 1654, which abolishes the Optical Media Board (OMB). This agency was originally established to fight piracy involving ...

item.title: Parliament discusses Iranian soccer players’ asylum and fuel security measures
item.description: During today’s sitting in the House of Representatives, Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke outlined the swift process through which humanitarian visas were granted to the Iranian women's soccer team, wh...

item.title: NLP Leader Calls for Transparent Audit of Nairobi Drainage Systems After Deadly Floods
item.description: Following the recent floods in Nairobi that claimed 26 lives and displaced around 800 households, National Liberal Party leader Dr. Augustus Muli is calling for a full public audit of the city’s drain...

item.title: Nairobi Medical Director Faces Court Over Alleged KSh11 Million SHA Fraud
item.description: A Nairobi-based medical director, Mishael Otieno Okumu, has been taken to court over allegations of orchestrating fraudulent claims exceeding KSh11 million from the Social Health Authority (SHA). Acco...

item.title: Security Firms Advised to Employ Only Trained Guards in Dodoma
item.description: Dodoma region security companies have been advised strongly to avoid hiring untrained guards just to save on salary costs. Wallace Lussingu, the new chairperson of the Tanzania Security Industry Assoc...

item.title: Acting Police Minister Says N2 Wall Won’t Replace Policing or Reduce Crime
item.description: Acting police minister Firoz Cachalia has stated that the proposed R120 million wall along the N2 in Cape Town is unlikely to reduce crime. The statement came in response to a parliamentary question f...

item.title: UK Political Figures Show Divergent Views on Involvement in Iran Conflict
item.description: The article reviews the contrasting positions of UK politicians regarding the US-led conflict with Iran. Labour leader Keir Starmer has opted for caution, advocating limited involvement due to the que...

item.title: Protests Erupt in Quezon City as Major Fuel Price Increases Take Effect Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: On March 10, 2026, various groups, including the peasant organisation Anakpawis and allied activists, staged demonstrations in Quezon City and other areas to protest a substantial oil price hike imple...
